content.description: We use relative pronouns in relative clauses to give more information about something without starting a new sentence.

content.subtitle: Use
items: who for people — What's the name of the man who created the Internet?
which for things and animals — The experiment which worked was the last one.
where for places — This is the town where Albert Einstein was born.
whose to show possession — That's the man whose sister discovered a new planet.

content.subtitle: Watch out!
notes: We can use who for animals when we give them a personality: Our dog, who's called Benji, is eight years old.
When there is a relative pronoun, do not repeat the subject/object. ✓ What's the name of the man who created the World Wide Web? ✓ This is the experiment which I'm doing at the moment.

content.description: Non-defining relative clauses give extra information. The sentence still makes sense without the relative clause.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To give extra information — This program, which is totally free, protects your computer against viruses.

content.subtitle: Helpful hints
notes: We use commas with non-defining relative clauses.
Carl, whose sister is famous, is a friend of mine.

content.description: Defining relative clauses give essential information. If we remove the clause, we don't know exactly who or what we are talking about.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To define who or what we are talking about — This is the TV which works. This is the TV which doesn't work.

content.subtitle: Helpful hints
notes: We don't use commas with defining relative clauses.
We can use that instead of who and which: Did you see the programme about the woman that invented Tippex?

sections.title: A: Complete with a relative pronoun
sections.instructions: Write who, which, where or whose in each gap.

items.sentence: Do you know the girl ...... lives next door?
This is the laptop ...... I bought last week.
That's the café ...... we usually meet after class.
I know a student ...... brother plays for the national team.
The teacher ...... helped me was very kind.
Is this the book ...... you were looking for?
They moved to a village ...... everyone knows each other.
The boy ...... phone rang in class was embarrassed.

sections.title: B: Defining or non-defining
sections.instructions: Add commas where needed. If no commas are needed, write 'No commas'.

items.sentence: My sister who lives in Dublin is coming to visit us.
items.answer: My sister, who lives in Dublin, is coming to visit us.

items.sentence: The car which is parked outside belongs to my neighbour.
Mr Lewis who teaches us maths is absent today.
items.answer: Mr Lewis, who teaches us maths, is absent today.

items.sentence: The students who studied hard passed the exam.
This film which won three awards is on TV tonight.
items.answer: This film, which won three awards, is on TV tonight.

items.sentence: People who smoke should try to stop.

sections.title: C: Use who, which or that
sections.instructions: Choose the best option in each sentence.

items.sentence: She's the teacher **who / which / where** helped me with physics.
This is the film **who / that / where** everyone is talking about.
Do you know a shop **that / who / whose** sells old records?
The woman **which / whose / where** car was stolen called the police.
That's the street **who / where / whose** my grandparents lived.
The mobile phone **who / which / whose** I bought is already broken.
He's the actor **which / that / where** won the BAFTA last year.
I visited the museum **where / whose / which** has the dinosaur skeleton.

sections.title: D: Join the sentences
sections.instructions: Rewrite each pair as one sentence using a relative clause.

items.sentence: I met a woman. She can speak six languages.
items.answer: I met a woman who can speak six languages.

items.sentence: We stayed in a hotel. It was near the beach.
items.answer: We stayed in a hotel which was near the beach.

items.sentence: That's the boy. His father is a pilot.
items.answer: That's the boy whose father is a pilot.

items.sentence: This is the town. I was born there.
items.answer: This is the town where I was born.

items.sentence: I bought a bike. It is very fast.
items.answer: I bought a bike which is very fast.

items.sentence: The restaurant is expensive. We had dinner there yesterday.
items.answer: The restaurant where we had dinner yesterday is expensive.

sections.title: E: Correct the mistakes
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: That's the man who he won the competition.
items.answer: That's the man who won the competition.

items.sentence: This is the computer which I use it every day.
items.answer: This is the computer which I use every day.

items.sentence: My uncle, who he lives in Canada, is visiting us.
items.answer: My uncle, who lives in Canada, is visiting us.

items.sentence: The book who I borrowed was very interesting.
items.answer: The book which I borrowed was very interesting.

items.sentence: The school where I studied there is now closed.
items.answer: The school where I studied is now closed.

items.sentence: My friend that she plays tennis is from Italy.
items.answer: My friend who plays tennis is from Italy.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word in bold.

items.sentence:
The girl is my cousin. Her bike was stolen yesterday. **WHOSE**
The girl ...... bike was stolen yesterday is my cousin.

This is the café. We had breakfast there. **WHERE**
This is the café ...... we had breakfast.

I bought a jacket. It is waterproof. **WHICH**
I bought a jacket ...... is waterproof.

The boy won the race. I told you about him. **WHO**
The boy ...... I told you about won the race.

Do you know the reason? She is upset for that reason. **WHY**
Do you know the reason ...... she is upset?

This is the park. I learned to ride a bike here. **WHERE**
This is the park ...... I learned to ride a bike.
The person ...... answered the phone was very helpful.
That's the house ...... roof is red.
This is the shop ...... I bought my trainers.
The film ...... we watched last night was great.
Do you know the girl ...... brother is in my class?
The village ...... my grandparents live is very small.
He's the student ...... got full marks in the test.
The laptop ...... I need is too expensive.

sections.title: H: Complete the text
sections.instructions: Write one relative word in each gap.

items.sentence: I have a friend ...... lives in London.
She works in a company ...... makes educational apps.
The office, ...... is near Hyde Park, is very modern.
Her manager is a woman ...... ideas are always creative.
The building ...... they work has a rooftop garden.
She told me the reason ...... she loves her job so much.
She has colleagues ...... come from different countries.
It's the kind of place ...... I would like to work one day.
